calendar.matters:
Petition For Rescission Of Revocable Trust And Reformation Of Grant Deed
Off Calendar May-02-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Yulissa Zulaica (via Court Call) and Amy Hespenheide for Patty Hsu-Kam and Andrew Kam

The Court sought clarification as to why the parties are requesting a court order for rescission when it is a revocable Trust. Ms. Zulaica stated that the Grantor and Trustee mutually agree to revoke the Trust, that one of the Grantors is a citizen of Australia, that if they revoke the trust it is a taxable trigger event from an Australian perspective, and that the parties were not advised about the tax consequences in Australia. Ms. Zulaica also stated that there is a parallel procedure in Australia, that they are requesting an order from California, that if the Trust is rescinded, it's as if the agreement never existed, made reference to page 12 in the petition (applicable law in re Bodger's Estate), and made reference to Probate Code Section 17200 and Probate Code Section 800, stating that she believes there is a basis for rescission, that she is happy to brief it further if necessary, and a Guardian Ad Litem is appointed in Australia for the children of the Grantor. Ms. Hespenheide made reference to page 13 of the petition (contract rescinding), stated that there is an agreement to rescind the Kam Trust to ab initio and separate the agreement to rescind and the Grant Deed. The Petition for Rescission of Revocable Trust and Reformation of Grant Deed is Taken Under Submission. Ms. Zulaica is to provide further briefing no later than 2 weeks from today.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
